steps.title: Set up the proportion using the given ratio
steps.content:
The ratio of blue to green markers is 7: 1. Since there are 42 blue markers, we can set up the proportion:
$$\frac{\text{blue}}{\text{green}} = \frac{7}{1} = \frac{42}{x}$$
where $x$ is the number of green markers we need to find.

steps.title: Cross multiply and solve
steps.content:
Cross multiplying:
$$7x = 42$$
Divide both sides by 7:
$$x = 6$$
A is correct.
